summary: No ship: R-6’s prose contradiction is fixed and the RED record matches red.sha/tree, but both r3 proof gaps still permit false GREENs. Simpler alternative: retain the production design and harden these two test contracts. Open question: none.

findings.title: Blocker: executor ownership can still go GREEN with broken workflow ordering
findings.body: B-1 now assigns local branch/commit preparation to the workflow and all remote actions to the executor. However, the committed call-site gate only checks for tool presence and absence of direct `git push`, `gh pr create`, and `RUN_ID`. It neither proves `git commit` precedes the executor nor rejects the workflow’s existing direct `scripts/auto-merge-or-fail.sh` call. An implementation can therefore pass all four witnesses while pushing the wrong HEAD or retaining remote mutation outside the executor.
findings.recommendation: Add call-site witnesses enforcing branch creation → bump-tag → commit → executor ordering and forbidding direct auto-merge invocation outside `ensure-bump-pr.ts`.

findings.title: Blocker: the exact-argv harness discards argument boundaries
findings.body: The plan claims the stub rejects every push argv outside the three contracts, but the red.sha stub records and compares `"$*"`, flattening argument boundaries. A call such as `git` with `['push', 'origin HEAD:refs/heads/<branch>']` matches the allowed flattened ledger line and passes `grep -Fx`, while real Git receives one invalid remote argument and fails. Equivalent false-GREEN partitions exist for rebuild and adopt, leaving live recovery stalled.
findings.recommendation: Validate `$#` and each positional argument independently or use a NUL-delimited ledger. Add negative witnesses for combined remote/refspec and combined lease/remote/refspec arguments, then re-pin red.sha and regenerate the RED verify-record.

next_steps: Strengthen the R-6 workflow ownership and ordering witnesses.
Make the R-7 harness preserve argv boundaries.
Regenerate the machine-owned RED record without manually running the regression suite.
